sourceMovie.title: Robots
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: People revisit Robots for the same reason it broke through in 2005: clear stakes, memorable faces, and a comedy rhythm that doesn't stall in the middle. Chris Wedge keeps the ensemble pointed in one direction. The animation and comedy blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Space Jam
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Robots clicked for you, Space Jam (1996) is a strong next stop: both trade in animation and comedy, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Robots?
faq.answer: Try Space Jam, Home, Minions: The Rise of Gru—each shares DNA with Robots while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Robots?
faq.answer: Robots (2005) is best described as Animation, Comedy, Family, Science Fiction. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Robots worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 6.5/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this animation lane.
